elements.title: an uninhabited island territory
elements.contents: ... that the Trump administration imposed a 10-percent tariff on an uninhabited island territory home mostly to penguins (examples pictured)?

elements.title: an American second-rate mare
elements.contents: ... that the first Japan Cup was won by an American second-rate mare?

elements.title: Stephen Jolly
elements.contents: ... that, according to Australian newspapers, Stephen Jolly was the only Westerner to address protesters at Tiananmen Square in 1989?

elements.title: Ninomae Ina'nis
elements.contents: ... that fans of Ninomae Ina'nis bought a billboard in Times Square to celebrate her "birthday"?

elements.title: Renovating Action Party
elements.contents: ... that a Salvadoran bishop threatened to excommunicate Catholics who campaigned for the Renovating Action Party?

elements.title: Fabian Wrede
elements.contents: ... that Fabian Wrede's family was granted a barony to honor his grandfather Henrik Wrede's sacrifice in giving his horse to Charles IX of Sweden during a battlefield retreat?

elements.title: Margaret Barker
elements.contents: ... that Margaret Barker performed in a play with her friend Katharine Hepburn while they were students at Bryn Mawr College?

elements.title: Seven Guitars
elements.contents: ... that Seven Guitars was the last collaboration between August Wilson and his long-time director?

elements.title: a diss track aimed at Suge Knight
elements.contents: ... that Dr. Dre dissuaded Eminem from releasing a diss track aimed at Suge Knight?
